channel.title: BBC Sport
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: Liverpool agree deal with Iraola to succeed Slot
item.description: Liverpool reach a verbal agreement with Andoni Iraola to take over as head coach following the sacking of Arne Slot.

item.title: Sir Kenny Dalglish undergoing treatment for cancer
item.description: Scotland, Liverpool and Celtic great Sir Kenny Dalglish confirms he is undergoing treatment for cancer.

item.title: No more delayed flag? Fifa adds new offside tech for World Cup
item.description: The delayed offside flag could almost become a thing of the past after Fifa unveiled new technology to be used at the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: Silva to leave Fulham as he closes in on Benfica move
item.description: Fulham manager Marco Silva confirms he will leave the Cottagers after five years in charge, with the 48-year-old close to agreeing a deal to take over at Benfica.

item.title: Keane v O'Neill: Celtic's big decision?
item.description: Martin O'Neill and Robbie Keane are reportedly the main contenders to become Celtic manager next season.

item.title: Konate close to joining Real Madrid after Liverpool exit
item.description: France defender Ibrahima Konate is in advanced talks with Real Madrid about joining the La Liga club on a free transfer.

item.title: England players will be ready for heat, says Henderson
item.description: Jordan Henderson urges England's players to expose themselves to the sweltering Florida conditions so they can acclimatise to the intense heat expected at the World Cup.

item.title: Does Southampton Spygate boss Eckert deserve a second chance?
item.description: BBC Sport explores the future of Southampton manager Tonda Eckert after he admitted his role in the Spygate scandal.

item.title: How Cornish miners brought football to Mexico
item.description: As Mexico prepares to become the first country to host or co-host the World Cup three times, BBC Sport looks at its Cornish footballing roots.

item.title: Salford sack Robinson after play-off final loss
item.description: Salford City manager Karl Robinson is sacked by the League Two club nine days after their play-off final defeat.

item.title: Gordon to be oldest player at World Cup but Gunn handed No 1
item.description: Scotland goalkeeper Craig Gordon will be the oldest player at the World Cup this summer, but the 43-year-old has not been handed the number one shirt.

item.title: Aston Villa intent on keeping Rogers - Wednesday's gossip
item.description: Aston Villa do not plan to sell Morgan Rogers amid interest in the attacking midfielder, Barcelona remain keen on signing Marcus Rashford from Manchester United and Real Madrid hold talks with Inter Milan right-back Denzel Dumfries.

item.title: Scotland players have not discussed Israel boycott
item.description: Scotland's players have not discussed boycotting their World Cup qualifying double header against Israel, says midfielder Christy Grimshaw.

item.title: Hinds withdraws from England squad with ankle injury
item.description: Defender Taylor Hinds withdraws from the England squad after suffering an ankle injury last week.

item.title: From non-league to Premier League... Coventry's 'unreal' pair
item.description: Sky Blues forwards Brandon Thomas-Asante and Ephron Mason-Clark have shared a similar path to the top flight.

item.title: Bellingham is No 10 in England World Cup squad
item.description: England's squad numbers for the World Cup have been announced - but how much do the choices hint at Thomas Tuchel's thinking?

item.title: Spanish mayor cancels DR Congo friendly over Ebola concern
item.description: DR Congo's international friendly against Chile in Spain next week is cancelled after authorities raise health concerns over the Ebola outbreak in the African nation.

item.title: Liverpool will be enormous step for Iraola - Sutton
item.description: The Monday Night Club discuss Arne Slot's sacking from Liverpool and whether Andoni Iraola is the right replacement.

item.title: Tuchel tackles questions from team of under-11s
item.description: England head coach Thomas Tuchel fields questions from under-11 players before his team head to the World Cup.

item.title: Watch as Celtic beat Rangers in Women's Scottish Cup final
item.description: Watch as a Morgan Cross goal is enough to give Celtic victory over Rangers in the Women's Scottish Cup final despite a red card for Emma Lawton.

item.title: Milner a remarkable person, says Brighton chief exec
item.description: Brighton chief executive Paul Barber calls James Milner a remarkable person - and says they tried to persuade the midfielder to postpone his retirement and stay on for another year.

item.title: The Isak saga which damaged two seasons
item.description: Alexander Isak's move from Newcastle United to Liverpool was the transfer saga of last summer. BBC Sport looks at how it ended up affecting both clubs.

item.title: Southampton owner will not sack Eckert over Spygate
item.description: Southampton owner Dragan Solak tells BBC Sport he will not sack Tonda Eckert, despite the "mistake" the head coach made when authorising a spying campaign against rival Championship clubs.

item.title: Gilmour admits struggle as World Cup dream taken away
item.description: Billy Gilmour admits "it's been a tough one to get my head around" after the Napoli midfielder was forced to drop out of Scotland's World Cup squad through injury.

item.title: Download your World Cup 2026 wallchart
item.description: Get your essential piece of kit now by downloading the Fifa World Cup 2026 wallchart.

item.title: Which clubs have the most players at the 2026 World Cup?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks at which clubs have the most players at the 2026 World Cup

item.title: Japan benefit from new World Cup substitution rule
item.description: Japan become one of the first beneficiaries of the new time-limited substitution rule as they score while opponents Iceland have just 10 players on the pitch.

item.title: Devenny not ready for the beach after latest trophy
item.description: Crystal Palace's Justin Devenny says he is not ready for the beach yet as he gets ready to face Guinea and France with Northern Ireland.

item.title: Watch Scotland put four past Curacao in World Cup send-off
item.description: Watch Scotland come from behind to beat 10-man Curacao in their World Cup send-off at Hampden.

item.title: How Slot went from champion to sack in a year
item.description: Just one year after lifting Liverpool's 20th league title in his debut season, Arne Slot has been sacked. BBC Sport looks back on how a dream start unravelled on Merseyside.

item.title: The Sunderland footballer setting record straight after 46 years
item.description: When Roly Gregoire quit football in 1980 after suffering a knee injury, he had faced so much racism that he could not bear to watch football for many years.

item.title: Spurs secure Premier League survival with win over Everton
item.description: Joao Palhinha's goal just before half time helps Tottenham overcome Everton to secure the result they needed to ensure Premier League survival on a day of nerve-shredding tension.

item.title: West Ham relegated despite win over Leeds
item.description: West Ham's 14-year stay in the Premier League comes to an end after they were relegated to the Championship on the final day of the season despite beating Leeds at London Stadium.

item.title: Arsenal beat Palace before lifting Premier League trophy
item.description: Arsenal start their reign as Premier League champions with a routine win over Crystal Palace in a party atmosphere at Selhurst Park.

item.title: Sunderland beat Chelsea to qualify for Europa League
item.description: Sunderland beat Chelsea to qualify for European competition for the first time in 53 years, securing a place in the Europa League at the visitors' expense.

item.title: Fernandes breaks Premier League record as Man Utd beat Brighton
item.description: Manchester United beat Brighton on the final day of the season, with captain Bruno Fernandes breaking the Premier League record for most assists in a single campaign.

item.title: Watkins & Villa spoil Guardiola's final Man City match
item.description: Pep Guardiola's 593rd and final game in charge of Manchester City ended in an emotional Premier League defeat as Ollie Watkins scored a double for Aston Villa.

item.title: Wars, sanctions and punishments: Iraq's 40-year World Cup wait
item.description: Iraq return to the World Cup with ambitions of delivering stronger performances and results than first time round in 1986.

item.title: World Cup 2026: How to follow on the BBC
item.description: The biggest World Cup in history starts on 11 June and there are more ways to follow it on the BBC than ever before.

item.title: The Braintree defender going to World Cup with New Zealand
item.description: Braintree Town defender Tommy Smith is looking forward to the "crowning glory of his career" at the World Cup with New Zealand.

item.title: How do Aston Villa improve after making history?
item.description: After Europa League success capped their best season for decades, how will Aston Villa move to the next level?

item.title: Shankland contract gamble paid off for Hearts - Jones
item.description: Lawrence Shankland left Hearts to join Rangers within days of their runners-up finish in the Scottish Premiership.

item.title: Khusanov named in Uzbekistan's World Cup squad
item.description: Manchester City defender Abdukodir Khusanov makes Uzbekistan's squad for the World Cup, led by former World Cup winner Fabio Cannavaro.

item.title: Quiz: Name every city hosting a World Cup 2026 match
item.description: Sixteen cities were chosen to host World Cup matches this summer, can you name them all?

item.title: Introducing Shorts on the BBC Sport app
item.description: From stunning goals and shocking interviews to unforgettable celebrations, some sporting moments are made to be watched, not just read about - and that's why BBC Sport are launching Shorts.

item.title: Overcoming resistance - Ancelotti's bid to revive Brazil
item.description: Carlo Ancelotti had to win over the Brazilian public and restore calm to chaos as he bids to return the country to World Cup glory.

item.title: The greatest World Cup shirts - and what makes them iconic
item.description: What are the best ever World Cup shirts and what makes them iconic? BBC Sport ranks the all-time top 10 here.

item.title: Who should be your World Cup second team?
item.description: The World Cup is almost here and you will no doubt have mapped out your team's potential route to the final, but if they exit early who should be your second team to follow at the tournament?

item.title: Quiz: Name every nation to have played at a World Cup
item.description: Test whether you are able to name every team to have ever participated in a World Cup.

item.title: World Cup 2026: All 48 squads confirmed
item.description: All 48 confirmed squads for the 2026 World Cup in the US, Canada and Mexico.

item.title: All done deals since February, including deadline day
item.description: Check out the significant signings and departures in the Premier League, Scottish Premiership, EFL and Women's Super League.

item.title: How much prize money have Premier League teams earned in Europe?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains how much Arsenal, Aston Villa and Crystal Palace have earned in Europe this season

item.title: Which club received most red cards in a Premier League season?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything looks at the teams who have received the most red cards in a Premier League season.

item.title: Who won the Premier League golden boot?
item.description: BBC's Ask Me Anything team explains who has won the Premier League golden boot and other individual awards.

item.title: What would Crystal Palace winning Conference League mean for English clubs in Europe?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains how Crystal Palace winning the Conference League would effect English teams entering Europe next season

item.title: Who are the oldest players to feature at a World Cup?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks at who the oldest players are in the history of the World Cup

item.title: Who has qualified for next season's Champions League?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains which teams have already qualified for the 2026-27 edition of the Champions League.

item.title: Who has the most Premier League assists in a season?
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team look at who has provided the most assists in the history of the Premier League.

item.title: Six more arrests made over Rangers v Celtic fan disorder
item.description: A total of 37 arrests have now been made in connection with scenes at the end of the match on 8 March.

item.title: Earps & Leon poised to join London City Lionesses
item.description: Former England goalkeeper Mary Earps and Spain defender Mapi Leon are on the verge of joining London City Lionesses this summer.

item.title: Wales' Cain leaves Leicester after WSL relegation
item.description: Wales forward Hannah Cain is leaving Leicester City following the club's relegation from the WSL but international team-mate Olivia Clarke will stay.

item.title: Chelsea sign McCabe after Arsenal exit
item.description: Chelsea confirm the signing of Republic of Ireland captain Katie McCabe following her exit from Arsenal.

item.title: SWPL winners Hearts appoint Thomson as head coach
item.description: Andy Thomson is appointed head coach of Scottish Women Premier League champions Hearts, replacing Eva Olid.

item.title: Williamson to miss World Cup qualifier against Spain
item.description: England captain Leah Williamson is ruled out of the Women's World Cup qualifying matches against Spain and Ukraine.

item.title: How Toone is navigating grief through football
item.description: England and Manchester United midfielder Ella Toone opens up about grief, football and preparing for her wedding without her dad in a new BBC documentary.

item.title: The summer a golden generation lost its shine
item.description: The summer of 2006 was meant to be England's moment. A world-class squad, celebrity partners and confident fans - the hype was huge and the fall was hard.

item.title: When football turned to penalties to end 'cruel' system
item.description: No-one knew what to expect at football's first official penalty shootout - and no-one wanted to go down in history as the first to miss.

item.title: Frank's downfall: Inside his struggle to get players and fans onboard
item.description: Thomas Frank has been sacked as Tottenham manager after just eight months in charge. BBC Sport's Sami Mokbel takes a closer look at his tenure.

item.title: Cages, crushes and stabbings - is European away safety getting worse?
item.description: BBC Sport explores the safety issues fans face when following their team across Europe.

item.title: The great number nine decline - where have England's strikers gone?
item.description: BBC Sport takes an in-depth look at why England lack centre-forward options behind Harry Kane - a far cry from when the likes of Les Ferdinand only won 17 caps.

item.title: Captain, player, manager, Mister, number one - Davids at Barnet
item.description: BBC Sport remembers Edgar Davids' 15 months as Barnet player-manager in League Two and the Conference.
